title: marsilea mollis
intro: Marsilea mollis is a species of aquatic plant, also known as the water clover or four-leaf clover, which belongs to the Marsileaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Marsilea mollis include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and improving digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy. Marsilea mollis can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can be used in salads or as a garnish, although it is essential to note that its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
